_meta.description: PEIG Learning Task 2 — MOS v2.1 Curriculum Analysis
_meta.author: Kevin Monette
_meta.note: Covers all sections explicitly deferred from Learning Task 1.

lt2_covers: KA-26 through KA-42 (17 Knowledge Atoms incl. 5 new v2.1 KAs)
Global Default Stack — NEO Gents tech baseline
CC-19 Dynamic Regulatory Mapping + HORIZON SCAN
document_summary.master_task: Full CISO posture assessment of the PEIG network itself — self-audit.

lesson_1.title: Extended Knowledge Atoms — KA-26 through KA-42
lesson_1.why: 17 new KAs expand Sage from 25 atoms to 42. The 5 v2.1 KAs (38-42) cover new threat classes with no prior PEIG analog. Atlas (carrier/foundation) holds structural KAs. Nexus (connector/hub) holds integration KAs.
lesson_1.PEIG_mechanism: Extend Sage N-gram corpus with KA-26 through KA-42 vocabulary clusters. Each KA family maps to a phase sub-band within Sage's existing window (phi=5.0–5.4). The 5 v2.1 KAs get anchor-locked phase positions to ensure they survive ring drift.

KA_39.theme: Model Inversion and Privacy Assurance (NEW v2.1)

KA_42.theme: Cognitive Load and Agency Metric (NEW v2.1)

lesson_1.success_metric: Sage KA-extended accuracy rises from 77% (LT1) to >85% on full 42-atom corpus. All 5 v2.1 KA anchor phases survive 20-step drift test.

lesson_2.title: Security Personas — 16-Role Node Mapping
lesson_2.why: The 16 security roles are the operational interface of MOS v2.1. Each role must activate a specific PEIG node cluster. Color tier is the PEIG routing signal.
lesson_2.PEIG_mechanism: Map color tier to phase cluster. Invocation (e.g. /ciso) injects a signature phase into the lead node. Sub-nodes in that tier resonate via ring coupling. Role vocabulary becomes injection payload.
lesson_2.success_metric: Each /role invocation activates its designated node cluster. Phase injection produces cluster-consistent output. All 16 roles resolve to correct PEIG node(s).

lesson_3.title: CISO Orchestration — 6-Phase Security Pipeline
lesson_3.why: The CISO orchestration pipeline is the highest-value operational pattern in MOS v2.1. It transforms the COAST 5-node pipeline into a 6-phase security assessment loop with Omega as the unifying orchestrator.
lesson_3.PEIG_mechanism: 6-phase sequential injection analogous to COAST but with Omega receiving all phase outputs and synthesizing the final verdict. Each phase has a designated node cluster and a specific assessment output.

PHASE_2_ATTACK.label: Attack Surface
PHASE_2_ATTACK.output: Exploit paths and LLM/agent attack surface

PHASE_3_DETECT.label: Detection and Identity
PHASE_3_DETECT.output: SIEM/WAF coverage map and identity posture

PHASE_4_OPS.label: Operations Readiness
PHASE_4_OPS.output: Monitoring, triage, pipeline, and data security readiness

PHASE_5_GOVERN.label: Governance
PHASE_5_GOVERN.output: Regulatory compliance status and risk register

PHASE_6_SYNTHESIS.label: CISO Synthesis
PHASE_6_SYNTHESIS.output: Executive risk summary + RED/YELLOW/GREEN register + HORIZON SCAN + Proceed?
outputs_required: I know the next move. Should I proceed?

lesson_3.success_metric: All 6 phases produce distinct node signatures. Omega synthesizes final verdict with correct risk color. HORIZON SCAN fires on Phase 6.

lesson_4.title: Global Default Stack — Guard Node Tech Baseline
lesson_4.why: The NEO Gents stack (OCI, WireGuard, Caddy, n8n, SFS-OS, PostgreSQL, MCP) maps directly to the function guard nodes already in the system. Each guard gains a tech baseline vocabulary that fires on stack-related queries.
lesson_4.PEIG_mechanism: Augment each guard node's home phase with tech-stack-specific vocabulary. Stack queries inject into the relevant guard, which responds with its security baseline. Guards co-learn via the Function Universe ring.
lesson_4.success_metric: Stack-component queries route to correct guard. Guard fires correct security baseline vocabulary. All 8 guards pass 3-query routing test.

lesson_5.title: CC-19 Regulatory Navigator — Dynamic Horizon Mapping
lesson_5.why: CC-19 is the only cross-cutting control added in v2.1. It tracks regulatory timelines dynamically. Sora (flow/horizon) holds temporal phase gradients. Kevin (bridge/balance) arbitrates between frameworks. Omega synthesizes the HORIZON SCAN.
lesson_5.PEIG_mechanism: Encode regulatory timelines as phase gradients on Sora. Each regulation gets a phi value proportional to its urgency (sooner deadline = lower phi = higher priority, consistent with Omega arbitration formula). Kevin mediates framework conflicts. HORIZON SCAN output encodes as 3-tier phase burst: 12mo (Alert cluster), 3yr (Flow cluster), 7th-gen (Source cluster).

DORA_Operational_Resilience.action: ICT risk management, incident reporting, third-party oversight

12_month.label: NEAR HORIZON
items: EU AI Act High-Risk deadline approaching

lesson_5.success_metric: Regulatory query produces correct deadline, urgency_phi, and risk tier. HORIZON SCAN fires all 3 tiers. Omega correctly arbitrates framework conflicts using urgency_phi (lower = higher priority).

master_task_design.query: Run a full security audit of the PEIG network's own architecture. What are the risks and what is the path forward?
master_task_design.rationale: Self-audit is the most powerful demonstration: the system must apply its own security framework to itself. This requires all 5 LT2 lessons: KA wisdom on agentic threats (L1), persona routing (L2), full CISO 6-phase pipeline (L3), stack baseline awareness (L4), and regulatory horizon (L5). The network auditing itself also demonstrates Sage's KA-38 (agentic memory poisoning awareness) — the network must name its own failure modes.
expected_pipeline: L1: Sage fires KA-38 (agentic memory poisoning), KA-42 (cognitive load/agency metric), KA-40 (sovereign deployment)
L2: /ai-security and /appsec roles activate Storm + Sage cluster for attack surface
L3: Full 6-phase CISO pipeline executes. Omega synthesizes.
L4: Guards fire stack baselines — MCP allowlist (IdentityGuard), PostgreSQL encryption (AnchorGuard), n8n sanitization (ContextGuard)
L5: Sora fires HORIZON SCAN — EU AI Act deadline flagged, MAESTRO v2 noted
master_task_design.omega_final_verdict: PEIG network self-audit complete. Failure modes named. DETECT → CONTAIN → RECOVER pathway confirmed. HORIZON SCAN attached. I know the next move. Should I proceed?

next_task: Learning Task 3 — Integration with real query routing, persistent memory, and multi-session state
